name: Direct CP violation in K -> pi pi: Re(epsilon'/epsilon)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce Re(epsilon'/epsilon), the parameter quantifying direct CP violation in neutral kaon decays K -> pi pi. World-average experimental value Re(epsilon'/epsilon) = (16.6 +/- 2.3) x 10^-4 (PDG 2024). Lattice-QCD SM prediction has improved to (8.2 +/- 4.2) x 10^-4 (RBC/UKQCD 2020), bringing SM and experiment into ~1.5 sigma agreement.

regime.description: Neutral kaon system; direct CPV in K_L -> pi pi. Tests TeV-scale BSM CP-violating contributions to Delta S = 1 transitions.

current_bound.experiment: World average of NA48 and KTeV (PDG)
current_bound.reference: Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics', Prog. Theor. Exp. Phys. 2024, 083C01 (2024); RBC/UKQCD lattice prediction: Abbott et al., PRD 102, 054509 (2020), arXiv:2004.09440

what_it_excludes: Constrains BSM contributions to Delta S = 1 transitions: SUSY squark mixing, Z-prime, leptoquarks affecting kaon physics. Earlier ~3 sigma SM-experiment tension (around 2015) prompted many BSM proposals; the 2020 lattice update largely resolved this.

parameterization.name: Weak effective theory at scale m_c: Wilson coefficients of QCD penguin operators Q_3-Q_6 and electroweak penguin Q_7-Q_10.
parameterization.reference: Buras, RMP 1, 281 (1998); Cirigliano, Ecker, Neufeld, Pich, Portoles, RMP 84, 399 (2012).
parameterization.summary: epsilon'/epsilon depends on Im(C_4 + C_6 - C_8) at the kaon scale, evaluated with lattice matrix elements <pi pi| Q_i |K>.

procedural.bound_in_parameterization: BSM contributions to Im(C_4, C_6, C_8) at ~10% level, depending on flavor structure.
framework_inputs: Match framework onto Delta S = 1 effective theory at the EW scale; run down to m_c.
Use RBC/UKQCD lattice matrix elements for <pi pi| Q_i |K> in I = 0 and I = 2 channels.

primary_references.citation: Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics', Prog. Theor. Exp. Phys. 2024, 083C01 (2024)
Abbott et al. (RBC/UKQCD), 'Direct CP violation and the Delta I = 1/2 rule in K -> pi pi decay from the standard model', PRD 102, 054509 (2020)
